name: Brent Crude
summary: Day 69 Thursday. **BRENT COLLAPSED -7% MAY 6 ON AXIOS MOU SCOOP — TRADING $101.95 MAY 7 MORNING; SUB-$105 CHAIN DAY 1; SUB-$115 CHAIN COMPLETED.** Round-trip arc: May 1 $108.17 → May 4 $114.44 (kinetic restart) → May 5 $109.87 (Trump pause) → May 6 ~$103 (-7% MOU scoop) → May 7 morning $101.95 (+0.5% modest stabilization). The May 7 morning level is **$6 BELOW the May 6 morning level** — completing not just the May 4 round-trip but breaking BELOW pre-May-4 baseline. Goldman Sachs Q2 forecast cut to $90/bbl (from $99); World Bank April 28 forecasts Brent averaging $86 in 2026 (vs $69 in 2025) — 'largest energy price surge since 2022.' Institutional forecast band now $86-90 for 2026 average. JPMorgan's $150 overshoot retained ONLY in kinetic-restart scenario. **PAPER-VS-PHYSICAL BIFURCATION REMAINS THE STRUCTURAL INTELLIGENCE:** Hormuz transit volume still 5-6 vessels/day vs 138 norm; AWRP at 0.8-1% (still 6-8x pre-war baseline); CMA CGM San Antonio struck May 5 by cruise missile keeps kinetic-tail risk live. Asymmetric structure: peace-rally more priced than rejection-risk. **48-hour Iran response window closes ~May 8 evening — the binary test.** If MOU signs: Brent → $86-90 (Goldman/World Bank base case), another -10 to -15% downside. If Iran rejects: Brent +$8-15 within 24h, round-trip back to $115+ on kinetic-restart pricing. For India: $101 Brent compresses CAD pressure significantly vs $115; combined with USD/INR pulling back from ₹95.36 record + DXY weakness, India macro stress materially easing — but contingent on MOU signature.
